column2: "NHLA 90/50" refers to a hardwood grading standard that specifies a minimum of 90% heartwood on one face and 50% heartwood on the other. This is a color-sorting specification, most commonly used for cherry wood, to ensure a consistent, rich reddish-brown color with minimal sapwood
